content:
Hi. I want to reimplement the virtio-net driver in the rust. The related structs and functions are extensive. So I plan to only reimplement those in virtio_net.c and virtio_net.h for now. However, some structs defined in virtio_net.h are also used in other c files, e.g. virtio_net_config and virtio_net_hdr. Therefore I need to have a struct definition both on the c side and rust side.

It seems there are two ways to solve this problem. I can use bindgen to generate a '.h' file from rust, which is a reverse of the current usage of bindgen in r4l. The other way is to keep a '.h' file in c while rewriting the driver and use bindgen to generate the corresponding dedication on the rust side. Which one is a better solution?
Right now a .h file with a C struct + bindgen would be the solution we use elsewhere, so there's already good tooling support for it.
I will use this method. Thanks for your help!
Hongyu Li has marked this topic as resolved.
